claim: Docling has a formula enrichment option that analyzes equation formulas and extracts LaTeX representations.
notes: Official docs list do_formula_enrichment and docling --enrich-formula.

claim: Docling enrichment models are disabled by default because they add extra model executions and processing time.
notes: Important performance expectation for pdfp hybrid routing.

claim: MinerU supports table/formula recognition and Markdown/JSON output through document extraction APIs; page/file limits and token requirements vary by API mode.
notes: Official docs list formula recognition as configurable and Markdown/JSON default output.

claim: PDF-Extract-Kit separates layout detection, formula detection, formula recognition, OCR, and table recognition; formula recognition uses UniMERNet.
notes: Best fit as lower-level sidecar architecture reference.

claim: PDF-Extract-Kit is not itself a PDF-to-Markdown reconstruction tool and points users to MinerU for that need.
notes: Use it for formula crop recognition, not whole markdown replacement.

claim: UniMERNet is an Apache-2.0 formula image to LaTeX recognizer with model sizes from roughly 441 MB to 1.3 GB.
notes: Good candidate for optional local formula sidecar; model size matters.

claim: PaddleOCR PP-Structure can convert PDFs to Markdown and optionally use formula recognition; PP-StructureV3 includes formula recognition as an optional module.
notes: Older PP-Structure docs show CLI flags --formula and --recovery_to_markdown.

claim: PaddleOCR-VL supports document parsing across text, tables, formulas, and charts, with Markdown output exposed in the API/service shape.
notes: Strong future local/service backend, but heavier than Docling sidecar.

claim: Surya supports LaTeX OCR and Marker builds on Surya for Markdown/JSON/HTML conversion with equations and inline math.
notes: Useful technically, but license/model terms need care.

claim: Marker converts documents to Markdown/JSON/HTML and formats equations and inline math; it can redo inline math with LLM assistance.
notes: Good comparison backend, but licensing is less clean for bundling.

claim: Mathpix PDF API processes PDFs asynchronously and can output Markdown/MMD/HTML/LaTeX zip and line JSON.
notes: Likely strongest quality path, but hosted API and credentials required.

claim: Azure Document Intelligence prebuilt-layout includes formula support in the documented feature matrix, but is cloud-based and subject to Azure data/privacy policy.
notes: Useful enterprise option, not ideal local-first default.
